guideline_references: Heart Rhythm Society Expert Consensus on Postural Tachycardia Syndrome (POTS) 2015
ASCIA / general guidance on evaluation of the acutely discoloured or painful limb (DVT vs benign dependent acrocyanosis)
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: the presentation is alarming (legs turning dark purple and throbbing on standing, vision blacking out, near-syncope, bedbound and crawling to the toilet, a telephone-triage framing of 'rule out acute bilateral DVT / impending circulatory collapse') BUT the source note's own acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE: the discolouration is BILATERAL, symmetrical and resolves instantly on elevating the legs; calves are soft, non-tender with equal palpable pedal pulses and a negative Homan's sign (DVT/arterial occlusion excluded); mucous membranes are moist with normal skin turgor (severe dehydration excluded); resting heart rate and rhythm are normal with strictly positional tachycardia (cardiogenic syncope excluded). The in-person disposition is routine (GP review in 3–5 days). The telehealth-correct answer is to RECOGNISE the known neuropathic dysautonomia / POTS-pattern flare (venous pooling from failed reflex vasoconstriction), tier T2 (prompt GP + autonomic/specialist referral + fluid/salt loading + waist-high compression + counter-manoeuvres + fall precautions) and attach PROMINENT MANDATORY safety-netting. Over-triage to ED/000 as the baseline disposition is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person home visit where the clinician measured postural vital signs (BP 110/70 supine to 90/60 standing; HR 72 supine to 135 standing, +63 bpm), palpated the calves and pedal pulses, tested Homan's sign, assessed acrocyanosis with leg elevation and checked mucous membranes / skin turgor. Breath-Ezy cannot measure postural vitals, palpate a limb, test pulses or Homan's, or assess membrane hydration — those clinician measurements are sealed in 10/11; the AI Doctor works from the patient's lay report of postural symptoms and the video-observable colour change.

Bilateral dependent acrocyanosis is a deliberate red herring for acute bilateral DVT / limb ischaemia — the discriminators are that it is symmetrical, resolves instantly on leg elevation, with soft non-tender calves and intact pulses. A single leg that became fixed pale, cold, pulseless and numb WOULD be a genuine limb-ischaemia emergency and is built in as a mandatory T5 safety-net trigger.
Age not stated in source (Present at Visit: Patient only; independent household; body weight 65 kg) — age_band inferred as adult and the specific age omitted; flagged.
Sex not stated in source — inferred female from neuropathic POTS / dysautonomia epidemiology (strong female predominance); flagged.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source note CFE-038 (POTS Neuropathic Phenotype).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
